description: Explicit merge() calls fold SYNCHRONOUSLY inside a batch; only propagation defers. N calls produce N folds and one invalidation. This is pinned explicitly because 'does batching lose my merges?' is the accumulator case's first question, and the answer is no -- batching coalesces the downstream cascade, never the algebra. The distinction this fixture draws is between the fold and the notification: a binding that correctly defers invalidation until batch exit but ALSO defers the fold will show fewer merges than calls, and under a non-idempotent policy it will also land on the wrong value. Contrast merge_per_settled_cone_not_per_write.json, where a single merge is correct because the CALLER made one call -- there the graph decided how many ops existed. Here the caller decides, so the fold is exact. That is the same event-versus-state split the family already drew for RelayCell: exact when the caller counts the ops, flush-granular when the graph does. Contract: #lzmergefeed.

expect.note: an observer of the accumulator, so the invalidation count is visible
THE assertion: three calls fold three times (exact), but the watcher runs only once more (coalesced). A binding deferring the fold itself shows merges_of 1.
1+2+3 under Sum. A binding that folded only the last op lands on 3 -- the value catches what the count catches, because the policy is non-idempotent.
